mnemonics: JAKARTA SERVLET: Just Always Keep A Request, Then Answer. Servlet Executes, Returns, Views, Ends, Logs, Transforms.
keyPoints: Servlets are Java classes that handle HTTP requests and generate dynamic responses.
The core interfaces are Servlet, ServletRequest, ServletResponse, ServletConfig, and ServletContext.
The lifecycle is managed by the container: init(), service(), destroy().
commonMistakes: Not properly closing the PrintWriter and OutputStream objects, leading to resource leaks.
Mixing up the roles of ServletConfig (per servlet) and ServletContext (per web application).
Forgetting that the servlet is multithreaded and writing code that is not thread-safe.
